cases.question: Which 5 companies have the most total connections in our database — counting ownership relationships, news mentions, and industry category links combined?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  COUNT { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) }  AS subsidiaries_owned,
  COUNT { (:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(o) }  AS parent_links,
  COUNT { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }              AS news_mentions,
  COUNT { (o)-[:HAS_CATEGORY]->(:IndustryCategory) } AS category_links
WITH o,
  subsidiaries_owned + parent_links + news_mentions + category_links AS total_connections,
  subsidiaries_owned, parent_links, news_mentions, category_links
RETURN
  o.name              AS company,
  total_connections,
  subsidiaries_owned,
  parent_links,
  news_mentions,
  category_links
ORDER BY total_connections DESC
LIMIT 5

cases.question: Which company pairs appear in both positive and negative news stories? I'm looking for companies that generate mixed sentiment coverage together. Show the top 10 pairs.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o1:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2:Organization)
WHERE o1.name < o2.name
WITH o1, o2, collect(a.sentiment) AS sentiments
WITH o1, o2,
     size([s IN sentiments WHERE s > 0]) AS positiveCount,
     size([s IN sentiments WHERE s < 0]) AS negativeCount
WHERE positiveCount > 0 AND negativeCount > 0
RETURN o1.name AS company1, o2.name AS company2,
       positiveCount, negativeCount,
       positiveCount + negativeCount AS totalMixedArticles
ORDER BY totalMixedArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

cases.question: By industry category, give me a comprehensive picture: how many companies are in each category, how many news articles mention them in total, what is the average sentiment of that coverage, and how many subsidiaries exist across the group? Show the top 10 categories by total article count.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (cat:IndustryCategory)<-[:HAS_CATEGORY]-(org:Organization)
OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org)
WITH cat,
     COUNT(DISTINCT org)  AS companyCount,
     COUNT(DISTINCT a)    AS articleCount,
     collect(DISTINCT a)  AS articles
WITH cat, companyCount, articleCount,
     CASE WHEN articleCount > 0
          THEN reduce(s = 0.0, x IN [a IN articles WHERE a IS NOT NULL | a] | s + x.sentiment)
               / articleCount
          ELSE null END AS avgSentiment
MATCH (cat)<-[:HAS_CATEGORY]-(org:Organization)
OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH cat, companyCount, articleCount, avgSentiment,
     COUNT(DISTINCT sub) AS subsidiaryCount
RETURN cat.name                AS category,
       companyCount,
       articleCount,
       round(avgSentiment, 3) AS avgSentiment,
       subsidiaryCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

RETURN org.name        AS company,

cases.question: For companies with at least 5 subsidiaries, show the most positively-covered news article about each (if any), and list the names of companies that sit two levels down in their ownership hierarchy. Return up to 10 companies.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (parent:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(child:Organization)
WITH parent, count(child) AS subsidiaryCount
WHERE subsidiaryCount >= 5
WITH parent, subsidiaryCount
ORDER BY subsidiaryCount DESC
LIMIT 10
CALL (parent) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(parent)
  WITH a ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC
  LIMIT 1
  RETURN a AS topArticle
}
OPTIONAL MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(grandchild:Organization)
WITH parent, subsidiaryCount, topArticle, collect(DISTINCT grandchild.name) AS twoLevelsDown
RETURN
  parent.name          AS company,
  subsidiaryCount,
  topArticle.title     AS topArticleTitle,
  topArticle.sentiment AS topArticleSentiment,
  twoLevelsDown
ORDER BY subsidiaryCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Classify every company into one of four groups: those with both subsidiaries and press coverage, those with subsidiaries but no press coverage, those with press coverage but no subsidiaries, and those with neither. How many companies fall into each group?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) } AS hasSubsidiaries,
  EXISTS { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }             AS hasCoverage
WITH
  CASE
    WHEN hasSubsidiaries AND hasCoverage          THEN 'Both subsidiaries and press coverage'
    WHEN hasSubsidiaries AND NOT hasCoverage      THEN 'Subsidiaries only, no press coverage'
    WHEN NOT hasSubsidiaries AND hasCoverage      THEN 'Press coverage only, no subsidiaries'
    ELSE                                               'Neither'
  END AS group
RETURN group, count(*) AS companyCount
ORDER BY companyCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

WHERE NOT EXISTS { (leaf)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() }
RETURN [n IN nodes(p) | n.name] AS chain
LIMIT 5

cases.question: Find news articles about 'renewable energy' by searching article text for those keywords. For the top 10 most relevant results, also rank them by how closely the article content matches the topic by meaning — not just by keywords. Return the article titles ordered by a combined relevance score, highest first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
// $embedding must be a pre-computed 1536-dim vector for "renewable energy" — no literal form is possible
CALL db.index.vector.queryNodes('news', 200, $embedding) YIELD node AS chunk, score AS vectorScore
MATCH (a:Article)-[:HAS_CHUNK]->(chunk)
WITH a, max(vectorScore) AS semanticScore
WITH a, semanticScore,
     CASE
       WHEN toLower(a.text) CONTAINS 'renewable energy'                                    THEN 1.0
       WHEN toLower(a.text) CONTAINS 'renewable' AND toLower(a.text) CONTAINS 'energy'    THEN 0.7
       WHEN toLower(a.text) CONTAINS 'renewable' OR  toLower(a.text) CONTAINS 'energy'    THEN 0.3
       ELSE 0.0
     END AS keywordScore
WHERE keywordScore > 0
WITH a, semanticScore, keywordScore,
     round(0.6 * semanticScore + 0.4 * keywordScore, 4) AS combinedScore
RETURN a.title, combinedScore, semanticScore, keywordScore
ORDER BY combinedScore DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: Execution error: {neo4j_code: Neo.ClientError.Statement.ParameterMissing} {message: Expected parameter(s): embedding} {gql_status: 42001} {gql_status_description: error: syntax error or access rule violation - invalid syntax}

cases.question: Enrich every company record in our database with its average news coverage sentiment score — if a company has no press mentions, default the score to 0. Update the records in groups of 500 at a time so the database is not overwhelmed, and skip any individual company that causes an error rather than aborting the whole operation.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
CALL (o) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o)
  WITH o, coalesce(avg(a.sentiment), 0.0) AS avgSentiment
  SET o.avgSentiment = avgSentiment
} IN TRANSACTIONS OF 500 ROWS ON ERROR CONTINUE
RETURN count(*) AS companiesProcessed

cases.question: Which companies are getting really bad press right now? Show me firms that appear mostly in negative news coverage — articles with strongly unfavourable tone.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
WITH o,
     count(a) AS totalArticles,
     sum(CASE WHEN a.sentiment < -0.3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS stronglyNegativeArticles,
     avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment
WHERE totalArticles >= 5
  AND toFloat(stronglyNegativeArticles) / totalArticles >= 0.5
RETURN o.name                                             AS company,
       totalArticles,
       stronglyNegativeArticles,
       round(avgSentiment * 1000) / 1000                 AS avgSentiment
ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC
LIMIT 20
